framework_anchors.validation_status: 8 of 8 dominant shapes Bing-validated (tied with daily-blog for highest)

_____A_WRITER_AGENT_____: ===== A. WRITER AGENT COMPLIANCE WITH §4.7 RECIPE =====

A_writer_compliance.summary: Writer covers tax + financial mechanics well but does NOT enforce §4.7's 8-shape recipe, does NOT enforce §3.9 synonym coverage, and does NOT enforce §3.3/3.4 universal rules. The agent reads as a shape-agnostic 'good persona article' template, not a fan-out-aware page builder.

3.1_year_marker_injection.evidence: Writer mandates 2026 in meta.title and an H1 like 'Health Insurance for [Persona] in 2026'. No explicit rule that EVERY H2 referencing a numeric value must include the year. No rule that EVERY table caption must be year-tagged.
3.1_year_marker_injection.gap: Section heading year-marker rule (§3.1 line 5) not enforced

3.2_state_context_everywhere.evidence: Persona is not state-scoped. Acceptable to skip.

3.3_eligibility_household_size_table.evidence: Writer prompt cites FPL hh-1 ($15,960) and 138% hh-1 ($22,025) as anchor facts but never requires a structured householdSizeTable for income-based personas (uninsured-low-income, domestic-workers, gig-workers). gig-workers.json shows ONLY hh-1 + hh-4 inline; no full hh-size table. self-employed.json shows hh-1, hh-2, hh-4 but only inline prose. Neither emits a structured table.
3.3_eligibility_household_size_table.gap: CRITICAL. §3.3 explicitly requires hh-size table for any income-gating persona. This is the canonical Bing-citable artifact.

3.4_how_to_apply_section.evidence: No required field for `howToApply` block (enrollmentDates, numberedSteps, govStartingUrl, documentsNeeded[], commonDenialReasons[]). gig-workers.json has no enumerated apply steps; self-employed.json has 'How to Project MAGI' steps but no application steps with .gov URL + documents-needed checklist. Neither lists 'Common reasons applications get denied'.
3.4_how_to_apply_section.gap: CRITICAL. Entailment is the dominant variant for personas (50.2%) and 'how to apply' is the highest-volume entailment shape per §3.4.

3.5_comparison_framing.evidence: optionsOverview is a comparison table (X vs Y vs Z). Writer enforces it. Good.

3.6_authoritative_source_narrowing.evidence: Writer specifies primary sources (healthcare.gov, IRS, KFF, SBA). Min 3 sources required. But no inline body links to .gov anchors—all sources foot-listed. Matches universal pattern §2/4 audit finding (no inline body links).

3.7_named_program_lookup.evidence: Programs (ACA Marketplace, Medicaid, CHIP, COBRA, HSA, HDHP) used canonically. State-named brands (Medi-Cal, AHCCCS, etc.) not relevant for non-state personas. Form 7206 cited correctly.

3.8_eligibility_entailment.evidence: FAQs include 'How do gig workers get insurance', 'cheapest health insurance for gig workers', etc. But no structured eligibility block with `incomeThresholds + nonIncomeRequirements + commonReasonsDenied`. No explicit H2 'Income limits' or 'Do I qualify' phrasing in either page.

3.9_demographic_specificity_synonyms.evidence: WRITER PROMPT HAS ZERO MENTION OF SYNONYM COVERAGE. The word 'synonym' does not appear in the writer agent. Section 5.6 framework rule (referenced in §4.7) is not propagated. CURRENT_STATE_AUDIT.md §4.6 flagged this as 'THE biggest single audit finding'—still unenforced.
3.9_demographic_specificity_synonyms.gap: CRITICAL. This is the single largest writer-agent gap. Re-verified counts confirm: gig-workers.json has 0 'freelancer', 0 'contractor', 0 'rideshare', 0 'sole proprietor', 0 'independent contractor', 0 'lyft', 0 '1099-K' (and only 2 '1099' tokens).

3.10_table_chart_phrasing.evidence: Writer does not require 'chart' / 'guidelines' / 'by [dimension]' phrasing in table captions. self-employed.json HSA table caption is '2026 HSA and HDHP limits'—acceptable but not lookup-shaped.

required_h2_sections.spec: §4.7 lists: Coverage options for [persona], Subsidy + tax credit eligibility, Self-employment health deduction, HSA/FSA fit, State-specific programs
required_h2_sections.writer_enforcement: PARTIAL. Writer's required-fields checklist enforces optionsOverview + optionDetails (covers shape #1) and detailSections min 2 (covers tax angles). But it doesn't enumerate 'HSA/FSA fit' as a required section, doesn't require 'State-specific programs / stipends' as a section, and doesn't require 'Catastrophic plan eligibility' or 'SEP triggers' which are dominant shapes 7 and 8.
required_h2_sections.gap: Writer treats detailSections as free-form 'tax/financial + situation-specific' — §4.7 says EIGHT specific entailment-heavy shapes should map to H2s/FAQs.

shape_2_PTC_subsidy_eligibility.gig_workers: PARTIAL (Option 1 covers PTC; no dedicated subsidy section)
shape_2_PTC_subsidy_eligibility.self_employed: PASS (covers PTC + cliff explicitly, mentions ARPA expiry)

shape_4_self_employment_health_deduction.gig_workers: PASS (dedicated detailSection on Form 7206 + correct Schedule SE caveat)
shape_4_self_employment_health_deduction.self_employed: PASS (dedicated detailSection, Form 7206 + Schedule 1 line 17 specifics)

shape_5_HSA_FSA_fit.gig_workers: FAIL (zero mention of HSA, FSA, or HDHP anywhere)

shape_6_state_specific_stipend_program.gig_workers: FAIL (no state-specific section; no mention of CA, NY paid family leave, MA-specific programs, etc.)

shape_7_catastrophic_plan_eligibility.gig_workers: FAIL (zero mention of catastrophic plans, hardship exemption, under-30 catastrophic eligibility)

shape_8_persona_marketplace_SEP_triggers.gig_workers: FAIL (no SEP section; no enumeration of qualifying life events)
shape_8_persona_marketplace_SEP_triggers.self_employed: PARTIAL (Option 4 mentions 60-day SEP after leaving W-2; FAQ on COBRA mentions SEP. No dedicated SEP H2.)

section_4_7_recipe_status.shape_coverage_score: gig-workers: 1.5/8 dominant shapes covered. self-employed: 5.5/8 dominant shapes covered.

writer_strengths: Tax accuracy strong: explicit Schedule SE caveat that SE health deduction does NOT reduce SE tax (avoiding the most common LLM hallucination)
ACA cliff phrasing nuance baked in (don't write 'below 400% = subsidies' as binary)
2026 anchor facts comprehensive (FPL, HSA, HDHP, OOP, mileage rate)
Locked enums + atomic write + JSON validation = good craft hygiene
Forces every optionsOverview row to have a matching optionDetails entry (validator-enforced)
Mandates link to /event/ or /qa/ to avoid duplication
ctaTarget=screener default correct for persona pages
writer_critical_gaps: §3.9 / Section 5.6 SYNONYM COVERAGE not enforced. The word 'synonym' does not appear in the writer prompt. This is the prior audit's flagged §4.6 'biggest single finding' and remains unfixed.

§3.3 hh-size table not required. No `householdSizeTable` schema field exists. §4.7 shape #2 (PTC eligibility) and the eligibility-entailment universal rule (§3.8) cannot fan out without the canonical lookup artifact.

§3.4 howToApply block not required. No `enrollmentDates / numberedSteps / govStartingUrl / documentsNeeded[] / commonDenialReasons[]` schema. Persona Entailment is 50.2%—the dominant variant—and 'how to apply' is the top entailment shape.
§4.7 shapes #5, #6, #7, #8 (HSA/FSA fit, state-specific stipends, catastrophic plans, SEP triggers) not enumerated as required H2s. Writer's detailSections are open-ended.
Section 5.7 pronoun discipline not mentioned in writer or verifier (cf. MA-state which passes via gold-standard pattern).

Persona consolidation rule from CURRENT_STATE_AUDIT.md §3.3 / Section 2.4 cluster representative not encoded. Writer has no instruction to absorb synonym variants under one canonical slug. Queue still treats personas as 1:1 slug:JSON without consolidation guidance.
writer_minor_gaps: FAQ length spec '40-100 words' conflicts with framework Section 4.5 '80-150 words' (universal pattern #15 in CURRENT_STATE_AUDIT §2)
No mention of `<strong>` density target or markdown-bold mechanism (universal pattern #2)
No paragraph word-count rule (universal pattern #1: target 150-300; current pages run 50-110)
No requirement to include CHIP for persona-with-children even when persona is non-family (gig-workers does include CHIP—good—but it's incidental, not enforced)
Sources field doesn't require .gov-anchor inline body links—all references foot-listed
No instruction to use 'chart' / 'guidelines' / 'by [dim]' phrasing on tables (§3.10)

writer_edits_top_3.edit: ADD synonym coverage block to writer prompt (Section 5.6 enforcement)
writer_edits_top_3.spec: Add a STEP 3 sub-section: 'Synonym coverage — list 4-8 colloquial/legal/IRS-form synonyms for the persona in `keyTerms`, and ensure each synonym appears at least 2-3 times in body content (H1/H2/intro/FAQ). For gig-workers: rideshare driver, delivery driver, freelancer, independent contractor, 1099 worker, 1099-K filer, sole proprietor, Uber driver, Lyft driver, DoorDash courier, Instacart shopper. For self-employed: freelancer, consultant, contractor, sole proprietor, Schedule C filer, 1099 contractor, independent professional, solo practitioner. Verifier MUST check synonym density.'

writer_edits_top_3.edit: ENUMERATE the 8 dominant §4.7 shapes as required H2/section coverage
writer_edits_top_3.spec: Replace the loose 'min 2 detailSections' with an explicit checklist: every persona page MUST address shapes 1-8 either in optionDetails, detailSections, or FAQ. Required H2s: 'Coverage options for [persona]', 'Subsidies and premium tax credits', 'Self-employment health insurance deduction (if applicable)', 'HSA / HDHP / FSA fit', 'State-specific programs and stipends', 'Catastrophic plan eligibility and hardship exemption', 'Marketplace SEP triggers for [persona]'. Allow 'N/A' fallback only when persona genuinely has no angle (e.g., catastrophic for >30s).

writer_edits_top_3.edit: ADD universal-rules block: householdSizeTable + howToApply schema fields
writer_edits_top_3.spec: Add to the Persona schema (and writer required-fields checklist): `householdSizeTable` (required for income-eligibility personas: gig-workers, uninsured-low-income, domestic-workers, part-time-workers, stay-at-home-parents — hh-1 through hh-8 with FPL%, 138% FPL, 400% FPL columns) and `howToApply` (enrollmentDates string, numberedSteps array of 3-7, govStartingUrl, documentsNeeded[], commonDenialReasons[]). This unblocks both §3.3 + §3.4 universal rules in one schema migration. Validator must enforce when present.

queue_consolidation_finding.current_queue_state: _queue.json has only 2 self-employment-category personas: 'self-employed' (status: queued) and 'small-business-owners' (status: queued, distinct identity — has employees). The 6 redundant queue rows from the sheet have NOT been added to _queue.json yet. CONSOLIDATION DECISION IS PRESERVED BY OMISSION, NOT BY EXPLICIT WRITER LOGIC.
queue_consolidation_finding.writer_consolidation_logic: ABSENT. Writer has no Section 2.4 cluster-representative awareness. If those 6 rows ever get added to _queue.json (e.g., via a sheet sync), the writer would generate 6 duplicate pages. Add explicit guard: 'Before queueing a new persona, check existing canonical pages for synonym overlap. If overlap >50%, route to existing page synonym sweep instead of new write.'
queue_consolidation_finding.recommendation: Codify the consolidation decision in the writer's STEP 0 pre-flight: refuse to write if `slug` synonym-matches an existing verified persona page within a synonym whitelist.

verifier_followup: Verifier does not check synonym density. Add: count occurrences of declared persona synonyms in body, FAIL if any synonym <2 occurrences.
Verifier does not check for the 8 dominant shapes. Add: scan H2s + FAQs for required topic coverage, FLAG (don't auto-edit) if a shape is missing.
Verifier should warn on em-dashes in body content (currently only style mention is in writer).

D_strategic_note: Persona is one of the two highest-validated templates in the entire fan-out study (8/8 shapes Bing-validated, tied with daily-blog). The template has the strongest theoretical citation upside in the codebase. The writer agent currently realizes only ~19% of that upside on gig-workers and ~69% on self-employed. Patching the writer is the highest-leverage persona work because: (1) every future persona page (10 queued, 11 with deferreds) will inherit the patched recipe, and (2) the 2 existing pages can be regenerated cleanly once the writer enforces the rules. The patch order is: writer first, schema migration second, regen third, queue cleanup fourth.
summary_200_words: The persona writer agent passes craft hygiene (locked enums, atomic write, JSON validation, ctaTarget enforcement, 1:1 optionsOverview↔optionDetails) and tax accuracy (Form 7206, Schedule SE caveat, ACA cliff phrasing) but fails to enforce the FANOUT_FORMULA §4.7 recipe. The single largest gap is Section 5.6 / §3.9 synonym coverage — the word 'synonym' does not appear in the writer prompt, and the prior audit's flagged failure on gig-workers.json (0 freelancer / 0 contractor / 0 rideshare) remains unfixed today. Of §4.7's 8 Bing-validated dominant shapes, gig-workers covers 1.5 (19%) and self-employed covers 5.5 (69%). The writer also misses two universal rules (§3.3 householdSizeTable and §3.4 howToApply) that would unlock the dominant 50.2% Entailment fan-out for the entire template family. Top 3 writer edits: (1) add Section 5.6 synonym block with verifier-enforced density check; (2) enumerate the 8 §4.7 shapes as required H2 coverage; (3) add householdSizeTable + howToApply schema fields and validator hooks. Regen priorities: gig-workers HIGH (blocked on writer patch — will reproduce gaps otherwise); self-employed MEDIUM (selective patch acceptable). Queue consolidation logic should be codified in writer STEP 0 before any sheet sync re-introduces the 6 duplicate persona-variant rows.
